<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes using shared_ptr for proper lifecycle management
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);

    // Get the singleton ObserverGraph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);

    // Trigger value change on the target node
    target->valueChanged(true);

    // Return notification status and observer's call count
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <functional>
#include <iostream>
#include <mutex>
#include <set>
#include <shared_mutex>
#include <thread>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test thread manager registration and safety check
 *
 * @param enableSafety Whether to enable thread safety
 * @return bool Whether thread safety is enabled after operations
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    if (enableSafety) {
        threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    }
    return threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable with the initial value
    auto varExpr = std::make_shared<reaction::VarExpr<int>>(initialValue);
    
    // Create an atomic addition expression using the AddOp operator

    auto addExpr = reaction::BinaryOpExpr{varExpr, addOp, std::ref(addValue)};

#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test observer node naming functionality
 *
 * @param name Name to set for the observer node
 * @return std::string The retrieved name from the observer graph
 */
std::string test_observer_naming(const std::string& name) {
    auto observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto node = observerGraph.createObserverNode();
    observerGraph.setName(node, name);
    return observerGraph.getName(node);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return true if all batch operations succeed, false otherwise
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto node3 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();

    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    ObserverGraph& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Create batch ID
    BatchId batchId = graph.createBatch();

    // Step 4: Register nodes to batch
    graph.addObserverToActiveBatch(batchId, node1);
    graph.addObserverToActiveBatch(batchId, node2);
    graph.addObserverToActiveBatch(batchId, node3);

    // Step 5: Verify nodes are in batch
    auto observers = graph.getObserversInActiveBatch(batchId);
    if (observers.size() != 3) [[unlikely]] {
        return false;
    }
    if (!std::includes(observers.begin(), observers.end(), node1) ||
        !std::includes(observers.begin(), observers.end(), node2) ||
        !std::includes(observers.begin(), observers.end(), node3)) [[unlikely]] {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 6: Unregister batch
    graph.closeActiveBatch(batchId);

    // Step 7: Verify nodes are no longer in batch
    observers = graph.getObserversInActiveBatch(batchId);
    if (!observers.empty()) [[unlikely]] {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 8: Return success status
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test observer reset functionality
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);

    // Get the singleton ObserverGraph instance
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: observer observes target
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);

    // Notify the target to trigger downstream notifications
    target->valueChanged();

    // Verify observer was notified initially
    if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Record call count before reset
    int callCountBeforeReset = observer->getCallCount();

    // Reset the observer node
    observer->reset();

    // Notify the target again
    target->valueChanged();

    // Verify observer was not notified after reset
    if (observer->getCallCount() != callCountBeforeReset) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**

 *

bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // 1. Create observer nodes

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // Step 1: Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);

    // Step 2: Create observer nodes

    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {

    }

    // Step 3: Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 4: Add observers to watch target

        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }

    // Step 5: Notify target
    target->notify();

    // Step 6: Count notifications and verify
    int total = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        total += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() == 0) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }

    // Step 7: Return results
    return std::make_pair(total, allNotified);
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // 1. Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);

    // 2. Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.emplace_back(std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }

    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // 4. Add observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }

    // 5. Notify target
    target->notify();

    // 6. Count notifications and verify
    int total = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        total += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() == 0) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }

    // 7. Return results
    return std::make_pair(total, allNotified);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                           const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                           const std::string& observer2Name) {
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObCb(target, observer1);
    graph.addObCb(observer1, observer2);

    target->notify();

    int total = target->getCallCount() + observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    bool chainStatus = (observer1->getCallCount() >= 1) && (observer2->getCallCount() >= 1);
    return std::make_pair(total, chainStatus);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

PersonRecord::PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
    : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

const std::string& PersonRecord::getName() const { return m_name.getPtr()->get(); }
int PersonRecord::getAge() const { return m_age.getPtr()->get(); }

void PersonRecord::setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.set(name); }
void PersonRecord::setAge(int age) { m_age.set(age); }

std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    
    auto calc = reaction::Calc<std::string>::create(
        [this](const std::string& name, int age) {
            return "Name: " + name + ", Age: " + std::to_string(age);
        },
        person.m_name, person.m_age
    );

    person.setName(newName);
    person.setAge(newAge);
    
    return calc->getPtr()->get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_batch_execution` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variables
2. Create variable to track recomputation count
3. Create calculation
4. Perform non-batch updates
5. Record recompute count after non-batch updates
6. Create batch context
7. Execute batch
8. Record recompute count after batch updates
9. Return final sum and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================

 *

 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // Create reactive nodes
    auto sensorNode = std::make_shared<VarExpr>(initialValue);
    auto recCounter = std::make_shared<RecCounter>();
    auto filter = std::make_shared<ChangeTrig>(diffThreshold);
    
    // Create filter-triggered calculation
    auto filteredValueCalc = std::make_shared<CalcExpr>(
        FilterTrigOp([this](const NodePtr &node) {
            return node->get<double>();
        }, filter), sensorNode);
    
    // Create status calculation based on levelThreshold
    auto statusCalc = std::make_shared<CalcExpr>(
        [this](const NodePtr &node) {
            return node->get<double>() >= levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
        }, filteredValueCalc);
    
    // Link the filter-triggered calculation to the RecCounter
    filteredValueCalc->addObCb(recCounter);
    
    // Update sensor with first value
    sensorNode->update(firstUpdate);
    int countAfterFirst = recCounter->getValue();
    
    // Update sensor with second value
    sensorNode->update(secondUpdate);
    int countAfterSecond = recCounter->getValue();
    
    // Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusCalc->get<std::string>();
    
    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of RecordingNode::valueChanged
inline void RecordingNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) {
    if (g_batch_execute) [[unlikely]] {
        return;
    }
    m_notifications++;
}

// Implementation of RecordingNode::notificationCount
inline int RecordingNode::notificationCount() const {
    return m_notifications;
}

// Implementation of test_observer_state_rollback
inline RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    auto observer_graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    observer_graph.registerNode(node1);
    observer_graph.registerNode(node2);

    // Initial dependency: node2 observes node1
    observer_graph.addObserver(node1, node2);

    // Save the current state for rollback
    auto original_state = observer_graph.saveState();

    // Replace dependencies transactionally
    auto transaction = observer_graph.beginTransaction();
    try {
        // Remove all observers of node2
        observer_graph.removeAllObservers(node2);
        // Add a new observer node3
        auto node3 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
        observer_graph.addObserver(node2, node3);
    } catch (...) {
        // Rollback on failure
        transaction.rollback();
        throw; // Re-throw the original exception
    }

    // Trigger notification on node3
    node3->changedNoNotify();
    int count_after_second_binding = node2->notificationCount();

    // Execute rollback (restore original state)
    transaction.rollback();

    // Trigger notification on node1
    node1->changedNoNotify();
    int count_after_rollback = node2->notificationCount();

    return RollbackObservation{
        .notificationsAfterSecondBinding = count_after_second_binding,
        .notificationsAfterRollback = count_after_rollback
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/observer_graph.h>
#include <string>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of NamedObserverNode
class NamedObserverNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
private:
    std::string m_name;
public:
    explicit NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    const std::string& name() const { return m_name; }
};

// Implementation of ObserverCollectionSummary
struct ObserverCollectionSummary {
    std::size_t totalObservers;
    bool containsDirectObserver;
    bool containsTransitiveObserver;
};

// Implementation of test_observer_collection
ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    // Create nodes
    NamedObserverNode nodeA("A");
    NamedObserverNode nodeB("B");
    NamedObserverNode nodeC("C");

    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Register nodes into the graph
    graph.registerNode(std::static_pointer_cast<reaction::ObserverNode>(nodeA.shared_from_this()));
    graph.registerNode(std::static_pointer_cast<reaction::ObserverNode>(nodeB.shared_from_this()));
    graph.registerNode(std::static_pointer_cast<reaction::ObserverNode>(nodeC.shared_from_this()));

    // Set up direct observer relationship (A observes B)
    graph.addObserver(std::static_pointer_cast<reaction::ObserverNode>(nodeA.shared_from_this()),
                      std::static_pointer_cast<reaction::ObserverNode>(nodeB.shared_from_this()));

    // Set up transitive observer relationship (B observes C)
    graph.addObserver(std::static_pointer_cast<reaction::ObserverNode>(nodeB.shared_from_this()),
                      std::static_pointer_cast<reaction::ObserverNode>(nodeC.shared_from_this()));

    // Create NodeSet to collect observers of A
    reaction::NodeSet observersSet;
    nodeA.collectObservers(observersSet);

    // Check if direct and transitive observers are present
    bool hasDirect = !observersSet.getPtrList().empty() && 
                     observersSet.getPtrList().front()->getName() == "B";
    bool hasTransitive = !observersSet.getPtrList().empty() && 
                         observersSet.getPtrList().back()->getName() == "C";

    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        .totalObservers = observersSet.getPtrList().size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = hasDirect,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = hasTransitive
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <functional>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    BatchExecuteResult result;

    // Initialize reactive graph
    RegFunGuard g_reg;

    // Create reactive variables
    auto value = VarExpr<int>(baseValue);
    auto recomputeCount = VarExpr<int>(0);

    // Define a calculation that triggers recompute count increment
    auto calc = CalcExpr<int>([this](const VarExpr<int>& v) {
        REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
        ++(*recomputeCount);
        return v; // Return original value to maintain dependency chain
    });

    // Link calculation to value dependency
    g_reg.addObCb(calc, value);

    // Capture value by getting weak pointer
    auto shared_this = value.shared_from_this();

    // Record initial recompute count
    result.recomputeCountBeforeBatch = *recomputeCount;

    // Create batch operation targeting 'value' node
    BatchOp batch(shared_this);

    // Add update operations to batch
    batch.addOp([deltaA = deltaA](const NodePtr& node) {
        REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
        if (auto wp = node.lock()) [[likely]]
            wp->setVal(*wp->getVal() + deltaA);
    });

    batch.addOp([deltaB = deltaB](const NodePtr& node) {
        REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
        if (auto wp = node.lock()) [[likely]]
            wp->setVal(*wp->getVal() + deltaB);
    });

    // Execute batch operations
    batch.execute();

    // Capture results
    result.recomputeCountAfterBatch = *recomputeCount;
    result.finalValue = *value;

    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchLifecycleResult {
    bool closedInitially;
    bool closedAfterClose;
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute;
};

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    reaction::Var<int> reactiveVar(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    reaction::Var<int> recomputeCount(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation that increments recomputeCount on each evaluation
    auto calc = reaction::Calc<int>([&](const int& v) {
        recomputeCount.set(recomputeCount.get() + 1);
        return v * 2; // Example operation
    }, reactiveVar);
    
    // 4. Create Batch object
    reaction::Batch batch({reactiveVar, calc, recomputeCount});
    
    // 5. Check if batch is closed initially
    bool closedInitially = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 6. Execute batch
    batch.execute();
    
    // 7. Record recompute count after execute
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCount.get();
    
    // 8. Close batch
    batch.close();
    
    // 9. Check if batch is closed after close
    bool closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 10. Ensure calculation is evaluated (implicitly verified via recomputeCount)
    
    return BatchLifecycleResult{
        .closedInitially = closedInitially,
        .closedAfterClose = closedAfterClose,
        .recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCountAfterExecute
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 1. Get thread manager instance (done via getInstance())
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    thread_manager.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    
    // 4. Get thread count after registration
    auto thread_count = thread_manager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Get thread safety status before enabling
    auto before_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 6. Manually enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after enabling
    auto after_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset thread manager state again
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // 9. Get thread safety status after reset
    auto after_reset = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 10. Return the transition
    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        .beforeEnable = before_enable,
        .afterEnable = after_enable,
        .afterReset = after_reset,
        .threadCount = thread_count
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    int updatedSum;
};

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    // Create a mutable reactive variable
    auto mutableVar = reaction::createVar(baseValue);
    
    // Create a constant reactive variable
    auto constVar = reaction::createConst(constValue);
    
    // Create a calculation that sums both variables
    auto sumCalc = reaction::createCalc([=]() {
        return mutableVar->get() + constVar->get();
    });
    
    // Get the initial sum
    int initialSum = sumCalc->get();
    
    // Update the mutable reactive variable
    mutableVar->set(baseValue + delta);
    
    // Get the updated sum
    int updatedSum = sumCalc->get();
    
    return ConstVarResult{initialSum, updatedSum};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AlwaysTriggerResult {
    int recomputeCountAfterInit;
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite;
};

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {
    auto varExpr = VarExpr<int>(initialValue);
    auto counterVar = VarExpr<int>(0);

    auto calcExpr = CalcExpr<int, AlwaysTrig>([=]() {
        counterVar() += 1;
        return 2 * varExpr();
    }, varExpr);

    int recomputeCountAfterInit = counterVar();

    varExpr.set(initialValue);

    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = counterVar();

    calcExpr(); // Force evaluation

    return AlwaysTriggerResult{
        .recomputeCountAfterInit = recomputeCountAfterInit,
        .recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>
#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard;
};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    auto threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager->reset();

    bool safetyBefore = threadManager->isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountBefore = threadManager->getThreadCount();

    std::thread worker([this]() {
        {
            reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard g([this](const std::thread::id& id) {
                // Registration logic (if needed)
            });
            // Simulate work to allow the thread to run
            std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(100));
        }
    });
    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfter = threadManager->isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountAfter = threadManager->getThreadCount();

    return ThreadGuardState{
        .safetyBeforeGuard = safetyBefore,
        .safetyAfterGuard = safetyAfter,
        .threadCountBeforeGuard = threadCountBefore,
        .threadCountAfterGuard = threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    auto var = std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(initialValue);
    auto calc = std::make_shared<CalcExpr<int>>(std::function<int(){ return var->get(); }, IV::LastHandle);
    
    int initial = calc->get();
    var->set(updatedValue);
    int updated = calc->get();
    
    auto shared_this = var->shared_from_this();
    g_node_manager.closeNode(shared_this);
    
    int afterClose = calc->get();
    
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initial,
        .afterUpdate = updated,
        .afterSourceClose = afterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#pragma once

#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackMonitorReport {
    bool monitoringEnabledBefore;
    bool guardAllocated;
    std::size_t usageDuringGuard;
    std::size_t usageAfterGuard;
    double usagePercentage;
    bool nearLimitDuringGuard;
    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitorReport report{};

    auto& sm = StackMonitor::getInstance();

    // Step 1: Disable monitoring
    sm.disable();

    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    sm.initialize();

    // Step 4: Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = sm.isMonitoringEnabled();

    // Step 5: Create StackGuard in a scope block
    bool guardAllocated = false;
    {
        StackGuard<64 * 1024> guard; // Allocate 64KB guard
        if (auto ptr = guard.getPtr()) [[likely]] {
            guardAllocated = true;
            // Step 7: Get current usage during guard
            auto stats = sm.getStats();
            report.usageDuringGuard = stats.currentUsage;
            report.nearLimitDuringGuard = stats.nearLimit;
            // Step 9: Calculate usage percentage
            report.usagePercentage = (static_cast<double>(stats.currentUsage) / stats.totalStack) * 100.0;
        }
    }
    report.guardAllocated = guardAllocated;

    // Step 11: Get current usage after guard
    auto statsAfter = sm.getStats();
    report.usageAfterGuard = statsAfter.currentUsage;

    // Step 12: Disable monitoring again
    sm.disable();

    // Step 13: Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = sm.isMonitoringEnabled();

    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/core/id_generator.h>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Step 1: Create lambda function to generate large payload
    auto createLargePayload = [largeSeed = largeSeed]() {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = largeSeed + i;
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Step 2: Create small SBOResource
    SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallRes(initialSmall);

    // Step 3: Create large SBOResource
    SBOResource<LargePayload> largeRes(createLargePayload());

    // Step 4: Update small resource
    bool updateReportedChange = smallRes.update(updatedSmall);

    // Step 5: Get storage info for small resource
    auto smallStorage = smallRes.getStorageInfo();
    bool smallUsingSBO = smallStorage.is_sbo;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage = smallStorage.stack_usage;

    // Step 6: Get storage info for large resource
    auto largeStorage = largeRes.getStorageInfo();
    bool largeUsingSBO = largeStorage.is_sbo;
    bool largeInitialized = largeRes.is_initialized();

    // Step 7: Return status
    return SboStorageStatus{
        .updateReportedChange = updateReportedChange,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallUsingSBO,
        .smallStackUsage = smallStackUsage,
        .largeInitialized = largeInitialized,
        .largeUsingSBO = largeUsingSBO};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/conditional_shared_mutex.h>
#include <mutex>
#include <shared_mutex>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety;
    bool trySharedWithSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety;
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    ThreadManager& tm = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();

    ConditionalSharedMutex mutex;

    // Step 4: Attempt to acquire shared lock without safety
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety = mutex.trySharedLockNoSafety();
    if (trySharedWithoutSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }

    // Step 6: Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard in a scope block
    {
        ConditionalSharedLock<ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(mutex);
        // Step 7: Attempt to acquire unique lock without safety
        bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = mutex.tryUniqueLockNoSafety();
        if (tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }

    // Step 10: Enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety();

    // Step 11: Attempt to acquire shared lock with safety
    bool trySharedWithSafety = mutex.trySharedLock();
    if (trySharedWithSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }

    // Step 13: Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard again
    {
        ConditionalSharedLock<ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(mutex);
        // Step 14: Attempt to acquire unique lock with safety
        bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = mutex.tryUniqueLock();
        if (tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }

    return SharedMutexResult{
        .trySharedWithoutSafety = trySharedWithoutSafety,
        .tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety,
        .trySharedWithSafety = trySharedWithSafety,
        .tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(
    const std::vector<reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode> &codes) {
    std::vector<std::string> descriptions;
    descriptions.reserve(codes.size());
    for (const auto &code : codes) {
        descriptions.push_back(reaction::ReactionException::getErrorDescription(code));
    }
    return descriptions;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct Counter {
    int value;
    void add(int delta) noexcept { value += delta; }
};

struct PointerAccessSnapshot {
    int valueAfterFirstAdd;
    int valueAfterSecondAdd;
    bool pointerStable;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate pointer-style access for reactive structs.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the Counter
 * @param delta Amount added on each pointer invocation
 * @return PointerAccessSnapshot Snapshot capturing results after pointer operations
 */
inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    auto counter = std::make_shared<Counter>(initialValue);
    reaction::VarExpr<Counter> var(counter);
    
    auto ptr1 = var.getPtr();
    {
        auto c = ptr1.lock();
        c->add(delta);
    }
    int valueAfterFirstAdd = var.get()->value;
    
    auto ptr2 = var.getPtr();
    {
        auto c = ptr2.lock();
        c->add(delta);
    }
    int valueAfterSecondAdd = var.get()->value;
    
    bool pointerStable = ptr1 == ptr2;
    
    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        .valueAfterFirstAdd = valueAfterFirstAdd,
        .valueAfterSecondAdd = valueAfterSecondAdd,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    // Create reactive variable
    ReactVar<int> source(initialValue);
    
    // Create copy of source
    ReactVar<int> copy = source.copy();
    
    // Check validity before close
    ValiditySnapshot snapshot;
    snapshot.firstValidBeforeClose = !source.isClosed();
    
    // Check equality before close
    snapshot.copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    // Close the source node
    source.close();
    
    // Check validity after close
    snapshot.firstValidAfterClose = !source.isClosed();
    
    // Check equality after close with exception handling
    try {
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    React::Var<int> var1(0, firstName);
    React::Var<int> var2(0, secondName);
    React::Var<int> var3(0, thirdName);
    
    std::set<React::Var<int>> reactSet = {var1, var2, var3};
    
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& var : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(var.getName());
    }
    
    bool allUnique = reactSet.size() == 3;
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class ResetAwareNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : ObserverNode(std::move(name)), m_notificationCount(0) {}

    int notifications() const noexcept { return m_notificationCount.load(); }
    void clear() noexcept { m_notificationCount.store(0); }

private:
    std::atomic<int> m_notificationCount;
};

struct ResetNodeResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeReset;
    bool notifiedAfterReset;
};

ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName, const std::string &targetName) {
    auto source = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);

    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph->addNode(source);
    graph->addNode(target);

    source->addObCb(target);

    source->notify();

    bool notifiedBeforeReset = target->notifications() > 0;

    target->clear();

    target->reset();

    source->notify();

    bool notifiedAfterReset = target->notifications() > 0;

    source->close();
    target->close();

    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBeforeReset, notifiedAfterReset};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackAllocationSnapshot {
    bool smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithMonitoring;
    bool guardSucceeded;
    bool allocationBlockedAtLimit;
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrates StackMonitor::canAllocate behavior across monitoring states.
 *
 * @param largeSize Size used for testing large allocations post-monitoring enablement.
 * @return StackAllocationSnapshot A record of allocation outcomes at each test phase.
 */
inline StackAllocationSnapshot test_stack_monitor_capacity(std::size_t largeSize) {
    StackAllocationSnapshot result{};

    // 1. Disable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().init();

    // 3. Check allocations without monitoring (small and large)
    result.smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(1024);
    result.largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // 4. Enable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().enable();

    // 5. Re-initialize stack monitor with updated monitoring settings
    StackMonitor::getInstance().init();

    // 6. Check large allocation with monitoring enabled
    result.largeAllowedWithMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // 7. Create StackGuard in scope to enforce stack usage limits
    {
        StackGuard g([this](const std::string& msg) {
            // Violation handler (silently ignore for test purposes)
        }, largeSize);

        // 8. Guard creation implies success if no exceptions thrown
        result.guardSucceeded = true;

        // 9. Check if allocation is blocked at the limit during guard scope
        result.allocationBlockedAtLimit = !StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);
    }

    // 11. Verify runtime allocation is allowed after guard release
    result.runtimeAllowedAfterRelease = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override { m_notifications++; }
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }
private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications{0};
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // Step 1: Create owner and field nodes
    TrackingFieldNode owner("Owner");
    TrackingFieldNode field("Field");

    // Step 2: Get observer and field graphs
    auto &observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto &fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Register nodes to observer graph
    observerGraph.addNode(&owner);
    observerGraph.addNode(&field);

    // Step 4: Register field to field graph under object ID
    fieldGraph.addObjField(objectId, &field);

    // Step 5: Bind field to owner
    observerGraph.addObserver(&owner, &field);

    // Step 6: Trigger value change on field
    field.valueChanged();

    // Step 7: Check if owner was notified before cleanup
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete = owner.notifications() > 0;

    // Step 8: Clear owner's notification count
    owner.clear();

    // Step 9: Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph.clearObservers(&owner);

    // Step 10: Remove field from object ID in field graph
    fieldGraph.removeField(objectId, &field);

    // Step 11: Attempt to bind field to owner again
    observerGraph.addObserver(&owner, &field);

    // Step 12: Trigger value change on field again
    field.valueChanged();

    // Step 13: Check if owner was notified after cleanup
    bool notifiedAfterDelete = owner.notifications() > 0;

    // Step 14: Close nodes
    owner.close();
    field.close();

    // Step 15: Return cleanup result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{
        .notifiedBeforeDelete = notifiedBeforeDelete,
        .notifiedAfterDelete = notifiedAfterDelete
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        ++m_notifications;
    }
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }

private:
    int m_notifications{0};
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    CountingObserver co;
    auto s1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto s2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto s3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    reaction::ObserverGraph &g = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    g.registerNode(s1);
    g.registerNode(s2);
    g.registerNode(s3);
    s1->addObserver(co);
    s1->notify();
    int first = co.notifications();
    
    s1->removeObserver(co);
    s2->addObserver(co);
    s3->addObserver(co);
    
    s1->notify();
    int firstAfter = co.notifications();
    
    s2->notify();
    int second = co.notifications();
    
    s3->notify();
    int third = co.notifications();
    
    s1->close();
    s2->close();
    s3->close();
    co.close();
    
    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        .firstNotificationsAfterInitial = first,
        .firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = firstAfter,
        .secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = second,
        .thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = third
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/batch.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct DepthOrderingSnapshot {
    bool initialComparatorResult;
    bool shallowBeforeDeep;
    bool deepBeforeShallow;
    bool nullComesFirst;
};

/**
 * @brief Class representing a node for probing depth ordering in reactive batches.
 */
class DepthProbeNode : public ObserverNode {
public:
    uint16_t depth{0}; ///< Public depth value for ordering comparison.
};

/**
 * @brief Tests the ordering behavior of reactive nodes based on their depth values.
 *
 * @return DepthOrderingSnapshot A snapshot of comparison results at different stages.
 */
inline DepthOrderingSnapshot test_observer_depth_ordering() {
    auto thread_manager = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager->reset();
    thread_manager->enableThreadSafety();

    auto shallow = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    shallow->depth = 1;
    auto deep = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    deep->depth = 2;

    NodeWeak s{shallow};
    NodeWeak d{deep};

    BatchCompare<ObserverNode> batch;
    bool initialResult = batch.compare(s, d);

    shallow->depth = 3;
    deep->depth = 2;
    bool shallowBeforeDeep = batch.compare(s, d);

    shallow->depth = 1;
    deep->depth = 0;
    bool deepBeforeShallow = batch.compare(d, s);

    NodeWeak nullPtr{nullptr};
    bool nullComesFirst = batch.compare(nullPtr, s);

    return DepthOrderingSnapshot{
        .initialComparatorResult = initialResult,
        .shallowBeforeDeep = shallowBeforeDeep,
        .deepBeforeShallow = deepBeforeShallow,
        .nullComesFirst = nullComesFirst
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UnaryExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip;
    int bitwiseNotValue;
};

inline UnaryExpressionSnapshot test_unary_expression_builders(int a, int b) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variables
    VarExpr<int> varA(a);
    VarExpr<int> varB(b);

    // Step 2: Build negated sum expression
    auto sumExpr = make_binary_op(AddOp{}, varA, varB);
    auto negatedSumExpr = make_unary_op(NegOp{}, sumExpr);

    // Step 3: Create reactive boolean variable
    VarExpr<bool> boolVar(true);

    // Step 4: Build logical NOT expression
    auto logicalNotExpr = make_unary_op(NotOp{}, boolVar);

    // Step 5: Create reactive integer variable
    VarExpr<int> intVar(5);

    // Step 6: Build bitwise NOT expression
    auto bitwiseNotExpr = make_unary_op(BitwiseNotOp{}, intVar);

    // Step 7: Get initial values
    int initialNegatedSum = expr(negatedSumExpr).get();
    bool logicalNotAfterFlipInitial = expr(logicalNotExpr).get();
    int bitwiseNotInitial = expr(bitwiseNotExpr).get();

    // Step 8: Update variables to trigger recomputation
    varA.set(a + 1);
    varB.set(b + 1);
    boolVar.set(false);
    intVar.set(3);

    // Step 9: Get updated values
    int updatedNegatedSum = expr(negatedSumExpr).get();
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip = expr(logicalNotExpr).get();
    int bitwiseNotUpdated = expr(bitwiseNotExpr).get();

    // Step 10: Return snapshot
    return UnaryExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialNegatedSum = initialNegatedSum,
        .updatedNegatedSum = updatedNegatedSum,
        .logicalNotAfterFlip = logicalNotAfterFlip,
        .bitwiseNotValue = bitwiseNotUpdated
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LogicalExpressionSnapshot {
    bool initialAnd;
    bool initialOr;
    bool updatedAnd;
    bool updatedOr;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate logical expression builders and operators on reactive values.
 *
 * This function shows how high-level logical operators (`&&`, `||`) compose reactive
 * expressions that are then lifted into `expr` calculations.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first boolean input
 * @param b Initial value for the second boolean input
 * @param c Initial value for the fallback boolean input (not used in this test)
 * @return LogicalExpressionSnapshot Snapshot of AND/OR results before and after updates
 */
inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {
    // Create reactive boolean variables from input values
    reaction::VarExpr<bool> varA(a);
    reaction::VarExpr<bool> varB(b);
    reaction::VarExpr<bool> varC(c); // Included per parameter spec, though unused here

    // Build logical expressions using reactive operators
    auto andExpr = varA && varB;
    auto orExpr = varA || varB;

    // Get initial evaluation results
    bool initialAnd = andExpr.get();
    bool initialOr = orExpr.get();

    // Update variables to trigger dependency propagation
    varA.set(!a);
    varB.set(!b);

    // Get updated results after variable changes
    bool updatedAnd = andExpr.get();
    bool updatedOr = orExpr.get();

    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,
        .initialOr = initialOr,
        .updatedAnd = updatedAnd,
        .updatedOr = updatedOr
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

template <typename Key, typename Value>
class IntCache : public CacheBase<Key, Value> {
public:
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::CacheBase;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::insert;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::lookup;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::getStats;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::invalidateAll;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::triggerCleanup;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    auto cache = std::make_unique<IntCache<int, int>>();
    
    auto initialStats = cache->getStats();
    GenericCacheStatsSnapshot snapshot{
        .entriesBefore = initialStats.entries,
        .firstLookupWasMiss = true,
        .secondLookupWasHit = false
    };
    
    auto firstResult = cache->lookup(42);
    snapshot.firstLookupWasMiss = !firstResult.has_value();
    
    cache->insert(42, 100);
    
    auto secondResult = cache->lookup(42);
    snapshot.secondLookupWasHit = secondResult.has_value();
    
    auto postInsertStats = cache->getStats();
    snapshot.entriesAfterInsert = postInsertStats.entries;
    snapshot.hitCount = postInsertStats.hitCount;
    snapshot.missCount = postInsertStats.missCount;
    
    cache->invalidateAll();
    cache->triggerCleanup();
    
    auto postInvalidStats = cache->getStats();
    snapshot.entriesAfterInvalidate = postInvalidStats.entries;
    
    const auto total = snapshot.hitCount + snapshot.missCount;
    snapshot.hitRatio = total > 0 ? static_cast<double>(snapshot.hitCount) / total : 0.0;
    
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/global_state.h>

namespace test_reaction {

GlobalStateSnapshot test_global_state_scoped_guards() {
    // 1. Reset global state
    reaction::resetGlobalState();

    // 2. Create initial snapshot
    GlobalStateSnapshot snapshot;
    snapshot.trackingActiveBefore = reaction::getInstance().isTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveBefore = reaction::getInstance().isBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveBefore = reaction::getInstance().isBatchFunActive();

    // 3. RegFunGuard in scope
    {
        reaction::RegFunGuard g([this](const NodePtr& node) {});
        // 4. Check state inside RegFunGuard
        snapshot.trackingActiveInRegGuard = reaction::getInstance().isTrackingActive();
        snapshot.batchActiveInRegGuard = reaction::getInstance().isBatchActive();
        snapshot.batchFunActiveInRegGuard = reaction::getInstance().isBatchFunActive();
    }

    // 5. BatchFunGuard in scope
    {
        reaction::BatchFunGuard g([this](const NodePtr& node) {});
        // 6. Check state inside BatchFunGuard
        snapshot.batchFunActiveInBatchGuard = reaction::getInstance().isBatchFunActive();
        snapshot.batchActiveInBatchGuard = reaction::getInstance().isBatchActive();
    }

    // 7. BatchExeGuard in scope
    {
        reaction::BatchExeGuard g();
        // 8. Check state inside BatchExeGuard
        snapshot.batchActiveInBatchExeGuard = reaction::getInstance().isBatchActive();
    }

    // 9. Reset global state again
    reaction::resetGlobalState();

    // 10. Check state after guards
    snapshot.trackingActiveAfter = reaction::getInstance().isTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveAfter = reaction::getInstance().isBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveAfter = reaction::getInstance().isBatchFunActive();

    return snapshot;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<think>Okay, I need to implement the test_thread_manager_force_register function using the reaction library's functions. Let me think about each step required.

First, the function needs to get the thread manager instance. From what I remember, the ThreadManager is a singleton, so probably there's a getInstance() method. So step 1 would be something like auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();

Next, reset the thread manager state. There's a reset() method, maybe. So tm.reset().

Let me check the reaction library's documentation. Oh right, ThreadManager has a forceRegisterCurrentThread() method which registers the current thread and enables thread safety if multiple threads are detected. But in this case, since we just have the main thread, maybe after this call, thread safety is still false. Because only one thread is registered. So after step 4, check again.

So step 5: safetyAfterMainForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled(). Since only main thread is registered, maybe it's still false. Then create a worker thread that registers itself. So create a thread that calls forceRegisterCurrentThread(). Then wait for it to complete.

    reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().forceRegisterCurrentThread();
});
worker.join();

Then after that, check the thread safety again. So safetyAfterWorkerForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled(). Now, since two threads have been registered, this should return true.

Finally, return a ForceRegisterSnapshot with these three values.

Putting it all together:

The function will look like this:

namespace test_reaction {
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();

    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread(); // Main thread registers
    bool safetyAfterMain = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    std::thread worker([this]() {
        reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    });
    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfterWorker = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    return ForceRegisterSnapshot{safetyBefore, safetyAfterMain, safetyAfterWorker};
}
} // namespace test_reaction

Wait, but the worker thread is created with [this] capture. But the function is inside a namespace, so 'this' might not be valid. Oh, but in this case, the lambda is inside an inline function, and since it's a non-member function, 'this' isn't available. So maybe the capture should be by getting the ThreadManager instance again. Alternatively, pass the ThreadManager instance into the lambda. Let me adjust that.

Instead of [this], perhaps capture by getting the instance inside the lambda. So:

std::thread worker([&]() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
});

Yes, that's better. Because the lambda captures the reference to the ThreadManager instance. So no problem.

So the code should be:

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <functional>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();

    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread(); // Main thread registers
    bool safetyAfterMain = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    std::thread worker([&]() {
        auto& worker_tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
        worker_tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    });
    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfterWorker = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    return ForceRegisterSnapshot{
        .safetyBeforeForce = safetyBefore,
        .safetyAfterMainForce = safetyAfterMain,
        .safetyAfterWorkerForce = safetyAfterWorker};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;
    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    VarExpr<int> varA(a);
    VarExpr<int> varB(b);
    VarExpr<int> varC(c);

    // Build sum expression: A + B
    auto sumExpr = make_binary_expr(varA, varB, AddOp{});

    // Build negated expression: -(A + B)
    auto negatedExpr = make_unary_expr(sumExpr, NegOp{});

    // Build chained expression: -(A + B) * C
    auto chainedExpr = make_binary_expr(negatedExpr, varC, MulOp{});

    // Capture initial values
    int initialSum = sumExpr.get();
    int initialNegatedSum = negatedExpr.get();
    int initialChainedValue = chainedExpr.get();

    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    varA.set(a + 1);
    varC.set(c + 1);

    // Capture updated values
    int updatedSum = sumExpr.get();
    int updatedNegatedSum = negatedExpr.get();
    int updatedChainedValue = chainedExpr.get();

    return RawExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialSum = initialSum,
        .initialNegatedSum = initialNegatedSum,
        .initialChainedValue = initialChainedValue,
        .updatedSum = updatedSum,
        .updatedNegatedSum = updatedNegatedSum,
        .updatedChainedValue = updatedChainedValue
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Implementation of the atomic helper for integer operations.
 * Tracks value changes and reports whether an operation caused a change.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial) : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        op();
        m_lastReportedChange = (oldValue != m_value);
    }

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op, bool forceNotify) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        op();
        if (forceNotify) {
            m_lastReportedChange = true;
        } else {
            m_lastReportedChange = (oldValue != m_value);
        }
    }

    int32_t value() const noexcept { return m_value; }
    bool lastReportedChange() const noexcept { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    std::atomic<int32_t> m_value;
    std::atomic<bool> m_lastReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Executes a series of atomic compound operations and records their effects.
 *
 * @param initial Starting value for the atomic operations.
 * @return A snapshot of the final value and change detection results.
 */
inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl atomic(initial);

    // Apply compound assignments and check change reports
    reaction::atomicAddAssign(atomic, 5);
    bool addChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();

    reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(atomic, 3);
    bool multiplyChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();

    reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(atomic, 0xF);
    bool andChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();

    reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(atomic, 2);
    bool leftShiftChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();

    reaction::atomicIncrement(atomic);
    bool incrementChanged = atomic.lastReportedChange();

    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        .finalValue = atomic.value(),
        .addReportedChange = addChanged,
        .multiplyReportedChange = multiplyChanged,
        .andReportedChange = andChanged,
        .leftShiftReportedChange = leftShiftChanged,
        .incrementReportedChange = incrementChanged
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
